verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin with dominant intracellular localization. However, multiple independent studies using western blot of isolated plasma membrane fractions, TEM-immunogold, and lipid-raft fractionation document a surface pool specifically in tumor/transformed cells (H292, A549, Daudi) that is absent in normal bronchial epithelial cells. This surface display is amplified by oncogenic transformation and inflammatory/stress states, involves active Golgi-dependent trafficking to lipid rafts, and represents an ecto-HSP60 pool on the outer leaflet. The mechanism is cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation / stress), not constitutive baseline surface expression.
